data.narrative:
Hubble Network is a Series B-stage space & aerospace company that has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of five private peers at the same or adjacent stage (Sierra Space, Cowboy Space Corporation, Varda Space Industries, Aalyria, and Skyryse) and six public Space & Aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL, and one additional ticker).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $100M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $190M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range from $1.1B (Skyryse) to $8.0B (Sierra Space). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target. The median peer valuation reflects positions across Series B and Series C stages, with Cowboy Space Corporation and Aalyria providing closest stage alignment at $2.0B and $1.3B respectively.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $162M to $1.7B, with a median of $845M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-to-stage progression and the significant valuation spread within the peer set, particularly the $8.0B outlier at Sierra Space. Key sensitivities include revenue trajectory, customer acquisition path, and capital efficiency relative to peers.
